title: Postmortem Action Item Closure
description: An incident-and-change response workflow that coordinates mitigation, approvals, and execution sequencing under operational urgency. Focus case: postmortem action item closure.
assumptions: Action items are tracked in a system that supports assignment, resource tagging, and closure states.
Approvals and reservations are exclusive: reserving a resource or approver removes it from the available pool until released.
Automation tools and runbooks are pre-registered and their availability is represented as initial facts.
risks: Delay in approvals or resource reservations can block closure and prolong incident recovery metrics.
Incorrect mapping of runbooks to action items may cause failed automated execution or require manual intervention.
External dependencies (vendors) may not respond in time, introducing additional closure latency or partial completion states.
